response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior full-stack engineer with 20+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and product software (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator work, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design. His low-latency systems work at Sociomantic and C/C++ experience at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but they fall far short of the deep hardware performance-architecture expertise this Principal-level role requires.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the years-of-experience bar
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components, robotic arms) plus recent AI-agent tooling work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware performance-architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect role

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload optimization background
